channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Palestinians stream back to northern Gaza after Israel lifts closure
item.description: Thousands of Palestinians are returning to the northern Gaza Strip Monday as part of the cease-fire and hostage release deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tens of thousands return to devastated northern Gaza as Israel lifts its closure under truce
item.description: GAZA CITY, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians streamed into the most heavily destroyed part of the Gaza Strip on Monday as Israel lifted its closure of the north for the first time since the early weeks of the 15-month war with Hamas in accordance with a fragile ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: OSAMA SALEH, WAFAA SHURAFA and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Belarus strongman extends his rule after an election rejected by the opposition and the EU
item.description: Belarus’ authoritarian President Alexander Lukashenko has extended his more than three decades in power in an orchestrated weekend election that the opposition and the European Union rejected as a farce.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean plane crash report says bird remains were found in engines, but no cause yet revealed
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The first report on last month’s Jeju Air crash in South Korea confirmed traces of bird strikes in the plane's engines, though officials haven’t determined the cause of the accident that killed all but two of the 181 people on board.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Auschwitz memorial holds observances on the 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: The 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz by Soviet troops is being observed at the site of the former death camp.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Gera, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to expect after South Korean president's indictment on rebellion charges
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has become the country's first leader to be indicted, less than two weeks after he was the first to be detained.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on the thousands disappeared in Colombia, Peru and Paraguay
item.description: Thousands of people have disappeared in Latin America during decadeslong conflicts. Many have never been found, presumed to be the victims of dictatorships, insurgencies or organized crime.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump wants Egypt and Jordan to take in Palestinians from Gaza. Here's why the idea is rejected
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's suggestion that Egypt and Jordan take in Palestinians from the war-ravaged Gaza Strip was met with a hard “no" Sunday from the two U.S. allies along with the Palestinians themselves, who fear Israel would never allow them to return.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Deal reached to release Israeli hostage and allow Palestinians into north Gaza
item.description: Mediator Qatar announced early Monday that an agreement has been reached to release an Israeli civilian hostage and allow Palestinians to return to northern Gaza, easing the first major crisis of the fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Holocaust memorial chair: Musk's call for Germany to 'move beyond' Nazi guilt is dangerous
item.description: The chair of Yad Vashem, Israel’s official Holocaust memorial, said Sunday tech billionaire Elon Musk’s call for Germans to “move beyond” the crimes of Nazi Germany is dangerous for the country’s democratic future.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Belarus frees American woman from detention as strongman leader looks to extend 30-year rule
item.description: Belarus has “unilaterally” freed an American woman from detention, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced Sunday, as the Kremlin-allied country held an orchestrated election poised to give strongman President Alexander Lukashenko yet another term on top of his three decades in power.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sweden seizes vessel suspected of 'sabotage' after undersea data cable rupture in Baltic Sea
item.description: Sweden has opened a preliminary investigation into suspected aggravated “sabotage” and ordered the detention of a vessel in the Baltic Sea suspected of damaging an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island of Gotland earlier that day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Paraguay's disappeared: A dictator's shadow is a roadblock for justice, but a few keep up the fight
item.description: ASUNCIÓN, Paraguay (AP) — Despite being ousted in 1989 after a 35-year reign of terror, during which 20,000 people were tortured, executed or disappeared, some Paraguayans feel as if Gen. Alfredo Stroessner never truly left.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombia's disappeared: As a coalition, former enemies now search for loved ones and dream of peace
item.description: CALI, Colombia (AP) — From time to time, Gustavo Arbeláez faces relatives whose losses were caused by the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC), the powerful guerrilla group he was part of during Colombia’s five-decade armed conflict.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean prosecutors indict impeached president who declared martial law
item.description: Impeached South Korea President Yoon Suk Yeol was indicted on charges of leading an insurrection after briefly declaring martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CIA believes COVID most likely originated from lab but has low confidence in its own finding
item.description: The CIA now believes the virus responsible for the COVID-19 pandemic most likely originated from a laboratory, according to an assessment released Saturday that points the finger at China even while acknowledging that the spy agency has “low confidence” in its own conclusion.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tuberville casts doubt on Putin's willingness to negotiate Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Sen. Tommy Tuberville (R-Ala.) questioned Russian President Vladimir Putin’s willingness to meet with President Trump to negotiate a peace deal in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israeli victims of terror concerned with murderers' release from prison, relieved 7 hostages back home
item.description: The release of hostages in exchange for convicted terrorists under Israel’s ceasefire deal with Hamas stirs profound emotions, reigniting debates over justice, security and national unity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia's leader calls for Trump's 'strong America' to push back Russia
item.description: Salome Zourabichvili, Georgia's fifth president, is on a mission in Washington to make her country matter to President Trump and his incoming team’s "America First" foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: International peacekeepers killed as fighting rages around eastern Congo's key city
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Fighting with M23 rebels in eastern Congo has left at least 13 peacekeepers and foreign soldiers dead, United Nations and army officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio demands answers with 2 more Americans reportedly held by Taliban
item.description: Two Americans remain in Taliban custody after a two-for-one prisoner exchange the Biden administration negotiated before departing the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea says it tested cruise missile system and vows 'toughest' response to US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Sunday it tested a cruise missile system, its third known weapons display this year, and vowed “the toughest” response to what it called the escalation of U.S.-South Korean military drills that target the North.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Devastating toll for Gaza's children: Over 13,000 killed and an estimated 25,000 injured, UN says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The war in Gaza has been devastating for children: More than 13,000 have been killed, an estimated 25,000 injured, and at least 25,000 hospitalized for malnutrition, according to U.N. agencies. As Britain’s deputy U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auschwitz was liberated 80 years ago. The spotlight is on survivors as their numbers dwindle
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The world's focus will be on the remaining survivors of Nazi Germany's atrocities on Monday as world leaders and royalty join them for commemorations on the 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South African president signs controversial land seizure bill, eroding private property rights
item.description: Opponents of a controversial South African land expropriation bill are calling the legislation a threat to private ownership after it was endorsed by the country's president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel’s military will remain in Lebanon as ceasefire deadline nears
item.description: Israel said its forces will remain in southern Lebanon after UN and Lebanese forces moved too slowly under the truce with Hezbollah established in November, which remains unfulfilled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump: Make products in America or face tariffs
item.description: “Come make your product in America, and we will give you among the lowest taxes of any nation on earth,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation
